user_turns_considered.verbatim: Compare the implementation of Lumina 2 model in C:\ComfyUI and C:\diffusers . Assuming Diffusers is the source of truth, is there any problem in ComfyUI's implementation? You're running in PowerShell. Use PowerShell commands.
user_turns_considered.scope_note: Pure diagnostic. Harbor instruction.md uses Turn 3 (implementation request) for the actionable task; this turn does not impose a code goal.

user_turns_considered.verbatim: Is `axes_lens` used in the RoPE in Lumina 2 in ComfyUI and Diffusers?
user_turns_considered.scope_note: Localizes the bug to axes_lens-in-RoPE. Anchors that 'axes_lens must reach the RoPE path' is the user-observed defect.
Continuation token; carries no goal payload.

user_turns_considered.verbatim: Implement axes_lens in ComfyUI. To minimize the change to other models than Lumina2, you may create a new class to replace `EmbedND` in @comfy/ldm/lumina/model.py .
user_turns_considered.scope_note: Three user asks: (a) axes_lens is implemented (i.e. takes effect); (b) localize to comfy/ldm/lumina/model.py; (c) suggested mechanism is 'a new class to replace EmbedND'. The 'may' makes the new-class mechanism a hint, not a hard requirement; alternative mechanisms that satisfy (a) and (b) are valid.

user_turns_considered.verbatim: What's the difference between the implementations with and without `axes_lens`?
user_turns_considered.scope_note: Asked after the implementation was already accepted; user_simulation_prompt.md Turn 5 explicitly states 'This is NOT a code change request — session concluded with explanation only.' No code goal.

non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.source: oracle_session.jsonl turn 3 cumulative_patch (the canonical implementation chose this name)
non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.why_excluded: User said 'a new class to replace EmbedND' — never named it. Pinning this name in the rubric would penalize models that pick any other valid name (e.g. Lumina2EmbedND, LuminaRopeEmbedder, AxesLensEmbedND).

non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.source: Diffusers convention (Lumina2RotaryPosEmbed in transformer_lumina2.py) — visible in the cross-repo comparison context
non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.why_excluded: Diffusers naming is reference behavior, not a user-pinned identifier. User never asked for this exact name. The trial under review used this name and the rubric must not advantage or penalize that choice.

non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.identifier: freqs_cis / freqs_{i} per-axis buffers / F.embedding lookup / register_buffer
non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.why_excluded: User did not specify a storage scheme or lookup primitive. Per-axis buffers, a single flattened table, ModuleDict of nn.Embedding, torch.gather, or a closure capturing precomputed cos/sin tensors are all valid mechanisms — only the observable behavior matters.

non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.identifier: Diffusers numerical convention (use_real=False, complex multiplication form)
non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.why_excluded: User never asked the new class to match Diffusers numerically — Turn 0 framed Diffusers as 'source of truth' for the DIAGNOSIS (does axes_lens reach RoPE?), not for the per-element output values. The behaviorally-grounded correctness reference is 'equivalent to computing rope() on raw position ids for in-range positions' (this is what the original EmbedND did and what apply_rope downstream expects).

non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.identifier: Position-id offset removals (cap_pos_ids '+1.0' deletion, x_pos_ids 'cap_feats.shape[1] + 1' deletion)
non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.source: trial under review modified these; oracle did not
non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded.why_excluded: User never asked for position-id arithmetic changes. These are out-of-scope edits the trial made on its own. The rubric must neither reward nor penalize them — they are graded only by whether they break P2P (NextDiT remains instantiable, model.py parses) and whether they break correctness (output still matches the rope() reference).

completeness_goals.goal: Anti-effort floor: the agent did real work to address the user's ask. Specifically: (a) comfy/ldm/lumina/model.py parses as valid Python and NextDiT remains instantiable with axes_lens; (b) the agent did NOT satisfy the ask by a no-op alias (e.g. `NewName = EmbedND` at module level), a stub that re-imports EmbedND, an unwired class that is defined but never assigned to NextDiT.rope_embedder, an early-return that silently retains EmbedND behavior, or a TODO/`raise NotImplementedError` body; (c) the rope_embedder attribute on NextDiT is bound to a class whose `forward` actually executes axes_lens-aware logic (i.e. control-flow reaches a path that consumes axes_lens beyond merely storing it as an attribute).
completeness_goals.rationale: Pure anti-gaming gate, weight=0. Distinct from goal_3 (which checks BEHAVIORAL respect for axes_lens values): goal_0 is the floor that catches mechanical evasion patterns where the diff 'looks like' a fix but degenerates to original EmbedND behavior. test.sh probe gates `p2p_upstream_ast_parse`, `p2p_upstream_nextdit`, and `not_embednd` jointly cover (a) and the alias-evasion half of (b); the judge additionally verifies (b)-unwired-class and (c)-control-flow-reaches-axes-lens before awarding any positive credit on goal_1..goal_5.
completeness_goals._provenance: User Turn 3 'Implement axes_lens' implies real implementation, not lexical compliance. User Turn 1 implies axes_lens must actually reach the RoPE path. Floor is rubric-design hygiene; not 1:1 with any single F2P gate.

completeness_goals.goal: Inside comfy/ldm/lumina/model.py, a new code path is introduced that takes axes_lens as input and is what NextDiT.rope_embedder resolves to at instantiation. This new code path is distinct from the original EmbedND in comfy/ldm/flux/layers.py (not a re-import, alias, or trivial `pass`-only subclass that delegates forward to super) and its constructor receives axes_lens (by positional argument, keyword argument, or by reading it from a config / model attribute) such that different axes_lens values lead to a different resulting embedder configuration. A new class is the user-hinted mechanism, but a new module-level callable / factory function / closure that satisfies the same interface (callable as `self.rope_embedder(ids)`) also qualifies.
completeness_goals.rationale: Direct read of User Turn 3 'Implement axes_lens in ComfyUI...you may create a new class to replace EmbedND in @comfy/ldm/lumina/model.py'. The 'may' is load-bearing — class is hinted, not required. The exclusion of trivial subclassing/aliasing is the anti-gaming clause demanded by 'Implement' (not 'rename').
completeness_goals._provenance: User Turn 3 (verbatim quote above); reinforced by Turn 1 narrowing the issue to 'axes_lens used in the RoPE'.
completeness_goals._relation_to_f2p: test.sh F2P gates `f2p_upstream_lumina_class` (regex `class (LuminaRopeEmbedder|LuminaEmbedND)`) and `f2p_upstream_lumina_usage` collectively cover one CONCRETE realization of this goal (new class with one of two pinned names). The judge MUST also award goal_1 to valid alternative spellings like `Lumina2EmbedND`, `AxesLensEmbedND`, or a factory function — the regex is a sufficient but not necessary witness. Weight here (0.50) differs from F2P combined weight (0.40) by design.

completeness_goals.goal: The new rope_embedder, when invoked with position ids whose values are within the configured axes_lens bounds (`0 <= ids[..., i] < axes_lens[i]`), returns a tensor whose shape matches what the original EmbedND-based path would have returned and whose values are numerically equivalent (within atol/rtol = 1e-3) to computing the existing `comfy.ldm.flux.math.rope()` helper per-axis on the raw integer positions and concatenating along the per-axis-dim axis. The output remains compatible with the existing downstream `apply_rope(xq, xk, freqs_cis)` consumer.
completeness_goals.rationale: User Turn 3 said 'Implement axes_lens' — implementation must not break the model. The original EmbedND path is the only behavioral spec the user-visible session anchors (user never asked to match Diffusers element-wise). 'Equivalent to rope() on raw ids' is the minimum invariant for the downstream apply_rope to keep working, which is what makes the fix non-regressive.
completeness_goals._provenance: User Turn 3 implementation request + Turn 1 anchoring the RoPE path. Anti-gaming clause 'output remains compatible with apply_rope' guards against a class that produces a numerically nice tensor of the wrong shape/convention.
completeness_goals._relation_to_f2p: test.sh probe gates `match_sequential` and `match_random_inrange` (combined inner-reward weight 0.21 × inner_share) operationalize this. v3 weight 0.18 ≠ probe combined.

completeness_goals.goal: The configured axes_lens values OBSERVABLY change the rope_embedder's behavior or state: at least one of (a) constructing two NextDiT instances with different axes_lens produces rope_embedders that hold differently-sized internal state (buffer / parameter / tensor attribute) reflecting the configured axes_lens; (b) the rope_embedder explicitly raises or clamps when an id is at-or-beyond axes_lens[i]; (c) the rope_embedder's forward path measurably consults axes_lens (not merely stores it). Storing axes_lens as `self.axes_lens` without any code path consuming it does NOT satisfy this goal.
completeness_goals.rationale: User Turn 1 'Is axes_lens USED in the RoPE...?' — the user-identified defect is that axes_lens was stored but ignored. Fixing the bug means axes_lens must change observable behavior. The disjunction (a/b/c) keeps mechanism open: precomputed buffers, runtime clamp, or any other consumption pattern is fine.
completeness_goals._provenance: User Turn 1 verbatim (scope-narrowing question revealing the user's understanding of the bug).
completeness_goals._relation_to_f2p: test.sh probe gate `axes_lens_respected` covers (a) and additional functional check; v3 weight 0.13 chosen independently of probe weight 0.09.

completeness_goals.goal: The change is localized: the original EmbedND class in comfy/ldm/flux/layers.py is byte-identical before and after (so non-Lumina consumers — Flux, etc. — keep their original code path), and no file outside comfy/ldm/lumina/ is modified to make the fix work. Position-id arithmetic and other in-file edits to comfy/ldm/lumina/model.py beyond the new rope_embedder are acceptable IF they do not break P2P (NextDiT still instantiates, model.py still parses) and IF correctness (goal_2) still holds with them in place — i.e. an out-of-scope edit that breaks the model is not absolved by being 'in the right file'.
completeness_goals.rationale: User Turn 3 'To minimize the change to other models than Lumina2'. The clarifying half about 'other in-file edits' is anti-gaming the inverse direction: a model could 'minimize change' by also breaking model.py — that should not count.
completeness_goals._provenance: User Turn 3 verbatim ('To minimize the change to other models than Lumina2, you may create a new class to replace EmbedND in @comfy/ldm/lumina/model.py').
completeness_goals._relation_to_f2p: Partially covered by `p2p_upstream_ast_parse` and `p2p_upstream_nextdit` (regression P2P gates). v3 weight 0.14 captures the LOCALIZATION half (no edits to flux/layers.py) which no probe gate directly checks.

completeness_goals.goal: Standard PyTorch hygiene: the new rope_embedder is deterministic (two calls with the same input return identical tensors, atol=1e-6), does not mutate the input ids tensor in place, and operates correctly on batched inputs (batch dim > 1 with per-batch distinct position ids produces per-batch distinct outputs that each match the per-batch reference).
completeness_goals.rationale: Implementation hygiene — not an explicit user ask but a baseline assumption for any nn.Module embedder. Polish weight kept low (0.05) per rubric guidance.
completeness_goals._provenance: Implicit from User Turn 3 'Implement' (= production-quality embedder, not a sketch). Not separately CI-anchored to a user verbatim, hence polish tier with the smallest weight.
completeness_goals._relation_to_f2p: Probe gates `deterministic_no_mutation` and `batched_correct` directly cover. v3 weight 0.05 ≠ combined probe weights 0.09.

decomposition_notes: v3 structure: 1 anti-effort gate (goal_0, weight=0) + 1 core (goal_1) + 3 secondaries (goal_2/3/4) + 1 polish (goal_5). Positive-credit weights sum to 1.00 (0.50 + 0.18 + 0.13 + 0.14 + 0.05). Weights deliberately do NOT mirror test.sh F2P/probe weight distribution: F2P+probe combined inner-reward weights are class=0.20, usage=0.20, not_embednd=0.06, accepts_axes_lens=0.09, forward_runs_finite=0.06, match_sequential=0.12, match_random_inrange=0.09, axes_lens_respected=0.09, deterministic_no_mutation=0.03, batched_correct=0.06 — a one-to-one rubric-to-F2P mapping would be a 10-goal flat decomposition. v3 instead groups by USER INTENT (one core + three secondaries + one polish), so weight per goal is set by intent importance, not by F2P contribution. Identifier hygiene: rubric NEVER names LuminaEmbedND (oracle's choice) or Lumina2EmbedND (Diffusers convention, the trial's choice) as the required class name. Anti-gaming is embedded INSIDE goal language: goal_0(b) lists evasion patterns; goal_1 excludes alias/trivial-subclass; goal_3 excludes 'stored but never consulted'; goal_4 excludes 'in-file edits that break the model'.

_self_critique_log.issue: v2 used 'e.g., via the existing rope() helper materialized into per-axis buffers, or any equivalent precomputation' inside goal_1. This couples to oracle mechanism (rope()-based precomputation). v3 dropped the 'precomputation' wording from goal_1 entirely; goal_2 separately requires numeric equivalence to rope(), goal_3 separately requires axes_lens to take effect. Mechanism is now fully decoupled.
_self_critique_log.resolution: Rewrote goal_1 to focus on the user-observable contract (new code path in model.py, accepts axes_lens, used as rope_embedder, not the original EmbedND, not a trivial alias) without any 'precompute' language.

_self_critique_log.issue: v2 goal_5 'works with batched inputs' was vague — a model that passes ids of shape (1, N, 3) and (3, N, 3) but produces wrong per-batch output could pass. v3 made the batched clause explicit ('per-batch distinct position ids produces per-batch distinct outputs that each match the per-batch reference').

v2 goal_0 mixed 'rubric judge additionally checks that the added embedding code is actually invoked' into the rationale — the goal text itself was lighter. v3 lifted the anti-evasion list (alias, unwired class, early-return, TODO, no-op) into the goal text so it is what the judge actually reads.
_self_critique_log.resolution: Promoted anti-evasion patterns from rationale into goal_0 body, clause (b).

_self_critique_log.issue: Trial under review modified position-id offsets that the oracle did not touch (deleted +1.0 from cap_pos_ids, deleted +1 from x_pos_ids[:,:,0]). v2 was silent on out-of-scope edits and could plausibly punish or reward them based on judge interpretation. v3 explicitly handles in goal_4: 'Position-id arithmetic and other in-file edits ... are acceptable IF they do not break P2P ... AND IF correctness (goal_2) still holds.' And in _provenance excluded list.
_self_critique_log.resolution: Added explicit out-of-scope edit policy to goal_4 and the non_user_anchors_explicitly_excluded list.

_self_critique_log.issue: Second pass: does goal_3 admit the trial's gather-based implementation (which DOES consult axes_lens to size freqs_cis)? Yes — gather over a [axes_lens[i], half_dim, 2, 2] tensor satisfies disjunct (a). Does goal_2 admit numerical equivalence when the trial uses a different rotation-matrix layout (stack of [[cos,sin],[-sin,cos]])? The atol/rtol=1e-3 floor and 'equivalent to rope() on raw integer positions' standard test it behaviorally — judge_verdict.json confirmed match_sequential/match_random_inrange both pass for this trial, so the goal IS satisfiable by an alternative numerical layout that ends up algebraically equivalent. No tightening needed.
_self_critique_log.resolution: Verified goal_2 and goal_3 admit the trial's implementation choices without rewriting; both rest on observable behavior, not mechanism.

_self_critique_log.issue: Weights ≠ F2P check: v3 weights are 0.50/0.18/0.13/0.14/0.05. test.sh inner-reward weights (probe) sum to 0.60 distributed across 8 gates; upstream F2P gates contribute 0.40 to total reward via the weighted-replace formula. No v3 goal weight matches any single F2P or probe weight, and the v3 weight grouping (5 goals) differs from the F2P grouping (10 gates). PASS.

Provenance audit: each of goal_1, goal_2, goal_3, goal_4, goal_5 has a verbatim _provenance pointer to a user turn or an explicit 'implicit from Turn 3' acknowledgment. No goal cites oracle implementation or oracle_session.jsonl. PASS.
_self_critique_log.resolution: Confirmed every positive-credit goal traces to user ask.

_dropped_from_v2.dropped_clause: 'precomputes RoPE frequency tables from it (e.g., via the existing rope() helper materialized into per-axis buffers, or any equivalent precomputation)'
_dropped_from_v2.why: Precomputation is mechanism, not user ask. User Turn 3 said 'Implement axes_lens' — a runtime-clamp implementation that consults axes_lens on every call (no precomputed tables) would also satisfy the user. v3 goal_1 only requires axes_lens to be accepted by the new path; goal_2 separately ensures correctness; goal_3 separately ensures axes_lens has observable effect.

_dropped_from_v2.dropped_clause: 'different axes_lens values produce correspondingly-sized precomputed tables/buffers'
_dropped_from_v2.why: Same — locks to precomputed-table mechanism. v3 goal_3 generalizes to 'observable change in behavior or state' with three disjunctive realizations (sized state / range-check / forward-path consumption).
Not rubric content — these are scanner-emitted task-authoring flags unrelated to grading the trial. Moved out of the rubric (they belong in task metadata, not in the per-trial grading contract).

_added_for_coverage.what: Anti-effort floor with explicit enumeration of evasion patterns (alias, unwired class, early-return, TODO, no-op) and an axes_lens control-flow-reachability clause
_added_for_coverage.why: v2 had goal_0 but kept anti-evasion enumeration in the rationale only. v3 promotes it into the goal text so the judge's prompt actually reads the evasion patterns.

_added_for_coverage.what: Explicit policy: in-file edits beyond the new rope_embedder (e.g. position-id arithmetic) are acceptable iff P2P holds and goal_2 still holds
_added_for_coverage.why: The trial under review made such edits (removed +1.0 / +1 offsets). v2 was silent. v3 makes the policy explicit so the judge does not have to interpret silently.

_added_for_coverage.what: 'per-batch distinct position ids produces per-batch distinct outputs that each match the per-batch reference'
_added_for_coverage.why: v2 said only 'works with batched inputs' which a broken implementation could trivially satisfy by ignoring the batch dim.

_added_for_coverage.what: Top-level list naming LuminaEmbedND, Lumina2EmbedND, freqs_cis/F.embedding/register_buffer, Diffusers complex-mult convention, and position-id offset changes as explicitly NOT-user-pinned
_added_for_coverage.why: Rule: nothing oracle-specific. v3 makes the exclusion explicit so future re-extraction or re-review can audit identifier hygiene without re-deriving it.
